name: DES-3B "Behemoth"
description: The Behemoth doubles the Leviathan's throughput by running dual superconducting bore tubes in parallel, sharing a common cryogenic bus and intake distribution system. Each bore is independently tunable — one can be optimized for coarse-grained regolith while the other handles fine dust fractions, improving recovery across the full particle size distribution. An integrated microwave pre-heater warms incoming regolith to 250 K, loosening adsorbed water molecules from grain surfaces and making them more responsive to the magnetic separation field. At 105 tonnes, the Behemoth is a major infrastructure investment requiring dedicated heavy-lift delivery, but its 1,042 kg/hr water output can sustain large permanent bases even at marginal dry sites. Power draw remains controlled at 6.5 MW thanks to improved high-temperature superconductor coils that operate at 40 K rather than the Leviathan's 4 K, drastically reducing cryocooler parasitic load.
